### Post by x3roconf on 2008-02-16

My system: 
acer al1721
gf 8800GT
ubuntu 7.10
My xorg configuration file

```

	Identifier	"stylus"

	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"nVidia Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Acer AL1721"

EndSection

	Identifier	"Default Screen"

	Monitor		"Acer AL1721"

#		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600""720x400""640x480"

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection


```

### Post by lswest on 2008-02-16
have you installed the restricted drivers?  do they work?  can you run ```
glxgears -info
``` in the terminal? (if you see the gears, it should be fine)**  If you want to force it to that resolution you can either install the nvidia-settings package ```
sudo apt-get install nvidia-settings
``` and run it with ```
nvidia-settings
``` then choose "automatic" by the screen resolution, and it should default to the highest resolution. 
If that doesn't work you can make a backup of your xorg.conf with ```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
``` then open the file with ```
gksudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
``` and delete all the resolutions except for the one you want, then close it and restart the Xserver with <ctrl>+<alt>+<backspace>.

**if you don't see the gears, go to system-->administration-->restricted driver manager and install the restricted drivers and change the line in the xorg.conf file from driver  "nv" to driver  "nvidia" **

---

### Post by x3roconf on 2008-02-16
I can't install any drivers by using restricted driver manager. I deleted other display modes than 1280x1024 but it still goes back to 640x480! :confused:

### Post by dayanandasaraswati on 2008-02-16
You try reconfiguring your Xserver. Just type this command in your terminal and follow the procedures
> sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

You'll get a blue screen with instructions to configure your monitor.  Follow the instructions given there. In any step if you are not sure what is to be done, do whats given there are default in the last lines of the instructions in every screen.

---

### Post by lswest on 2008-02-16
if the restricted driver manager doesn't work for you, you can try envy.  install it with ```
sudo apt-get install envy
```  might work for you^^  then launch it from applications-->system tools-->envy

### Post by x3roconf on 2008-02-18
I got it working when i changed 'nv' to 'vesa' :)

---

### Post by jespdj on 2008-02-18
> **x3roconf said:**
> I got it working when i changed 'nv' to 'vesa' :)
The 'vesa' driver is a generic video driver that uses only the basic resolutions and does not offer accelerated graphics. It will work, but the video might be slow, and you won't have fast 3D graphics and maybe playing movies will also be slow.

Download and install nVidia's latest drivers: [http://www.nvidia.com/Download/index.aspx?lang=en-us](http://www.nvidia.com/Download/index.aspx?lang=en-us)

---
